dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4312 IS: Federal Mechanical Insulation Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Cortez Masto (for herself and Mr. Daines) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources

form.official-title: To amend the National Energy Conservation Policy Act to include the installation of mechanical insulation property as an energy or water efficiency measure, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Mechanical Insulation Act of 2026.

section.header: Installation of mechanical insulation property as an energy or water efficiency measure in Federal buildings
section.text: Section 543(f) of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act (42 U.S.C. 8253(f)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by redesignating subparagraphs (E) through (H) as subparagraphs (F) through (I), respectively; and
by inserting after subparagraph (D) the following:
The term mechanical insulation property means insulation material, facing, or accessory product that—

subclause.text: in connection with a mechanical system; and

in a manner that meets or exceeds the minimum requirements of Standard 90.1 of the American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers (as in effect on the date of enactment of the Federal Mechanical Insulation Act of 2026); and

clause.text: results in a reduction in energy loss from the mechanical system.

paragraph.text: in paragraph (3)(A), by inserting , including identification of energy- and water-saving measures (including the installation of mechanical insulation property, if applicable), after a comprehensive energy and water evaluation.
